question:
Will Aston Villa win on 2025-11-09?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 9, 2025
If Aston Villa wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.premierleague.com/

evidences.title: Aston Villa Tactical Analysis | Build-Up Patterns, Winger Movements & Compact Defending
evidences.content: We’ll analyze how Villa structure their back four and central midfielders to progress the ball safely, how players like Morgan Rogers drop deeper to support the build-up, and how Pau Torres acts as a key pivot in moving the ball forward. You’ll also see how wingers and full-backs coordinate to create space, overload the flanks, and stretch the opposition. Defensively, we’ll explore Villa’s compact 4-4-2 shape, how they compress the pitch between the lines, and why they’ve conceded only 15 goals in the first 15 matches. We’ll also look at goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez’s incredible shot-stopping ability and his league-leading save percentage of 79.1%, which has been crucial to their defensive success. [...] the buildup in a more controlled way. When Rogers times his movement into deeper areas at the right moment and uses his first touch to take the ball into space, he's able to carry it forward effectively and drive into higher areas of the pitch, helping Aston Villa progress through the lines. After 15 matches this season, Aston Villa have generated an xG of 16.4. 45 but have already scored 22 goals resulting in a positive difference of 5.55. This clearly highlights their clinical...

evidences.title: Premier League 2025-26 preview No 2: Aston Villa | Football | The Guardian
evidences.content: Improving a strong squad is not straightforward, especially in the era of profitability and sustainability rules. Villa’s spine – spearheaded by Ollie Watkins – matches that of most division rivals; Harry Kane is the only Englishman to register more goals and assists in Europe’s top five leagues across the past five seasons. They begin the season at home to Newcastle, another team hamstrung by financial parameters, and have a seemingly kind start, facing only Eddie Howe’s side and Brentford from last season’s top 10 before the October international break. The warning sign is Villa flew out of the blocks last season – losing only one of their first 13 matches – but a hectic schedule punished them. Past five seasons ## The manager [...] There were plenty of positives, chiefly the continued emergence of Morgan Rogers, now one of the Premier League’s most coveted players, and the consistent class of Youri Tielemans behind him in midfield, and yet it was a campaign tinged with disappointment. Villa missed out on qualifying for Europe’s elite competition on goal difference after defeat at Manchester United on the final day and Emery’s hunt for silverware – he outlined his desire to wi...

evidences.title: 2025/26 Premier League Fixture Schedule Released
evidences.content: Copilot logo # 2025/26 Premier League Fixture Schedule Released GettyImages-2216972439 News Season kicks off with Liverpool hosting AFC Bournemouth at 20:00 on Friday 15 August, live on Sky Sports The Premier League has announced the fixture schedule for the 2025/26 season, which kicks off with Liverpool hosting AFC Bournemouth at 20:00 on Friday 15 August, live on Sky Sports. Following the opening night at Anfield, five more games will be shown live in the UK across the first weekend of the season. Aston Villa will face Newcastle United live on TNT Sports at 12:30 on Saturday 16 August with Sky Sports later that day showing Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester City at 17:30. [...] Owing to our clubs’ performances in Europe last season, a record nine Premier League teams will play in UEFA competitions in the 2025/26 season. While the Premier League celebrates this success, it will come with an impact on the scheduling of League matches. There is an increased likelihood of Premier League fixtures moving at relatively short notice, after our fixture announcements, should our clubs progress to the knockout rounds of these competitions. Supporters are asked to consider this when pla...

evidences.title: Aston Villa: Season Preview 2021/22 • Statsbomb Blog Archive
evidences.content: Want to read about another team? The rest of our Premier League season previews can be found here If you're a club, media or gambling entity and want to know more about what StatsBomb can do for you, please contact us at Sales@StatsBomb.com We also provide education in this area, so if this taste of football analytics sparked interest, check out our Introduction to Football Analytics course Follow us on Twitter in English and Spanish and also on LinkedIn This is an archive of the article which was originally published at: [...] but shoving it out of sight and out of mind. An improvement in the on-pitch performances powered the leap up the table: Villa turned a near-league-worst expected goal difference, -0.62 xGD per game, into a comfortable midtable process, bang on +0 xGD per game over the whole season. They improved at both ends of the pitch, adding 0.28 xG per game onto the attack (~10 goals a season) and shaving -0.34 xG per game off the defence (~13 goals a season). At the back, they conceded fewer shots (15.8 – 14.1) that were of a worse quality measured by xG per shot (0.10 – 0.09). They were defending better as a collective, with goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez having a st...
